QQQ.data_anomaly: QQQ price $708.93 is outside expected range ($250-$700). Verify data source — may be contract rollover, unit mismatch, or genuine historic move.

macro_summary: Monday's session reflected deepening bond market fears, with the US10Y yield spiking to 4.59% after the 30-year auction sold above 5% for the first time since 2007 (per Reuters, May 18). The S&P 500 fell 1.1% to 739, QQQ dropped 1.5%, and SOXX tumbled 3.9% as inflation and Hormuz-driven oil at $103.31 extended into a second month. Over the weekend, a US-China summit yielded tariff reduction signals (per CNBC, May 16) and China pledged to address rare-earth shortages (per Reuters, May 18), but chip export controls remain unresolved, leaving semis under pressure.
commodities_note: WTI held at $103.31 (+0.12%) as the Hormuz disruption entered its 4th week with new ship seizures (per AP, May 14). Gold eased 0.1% to $4,542, while the DXY softened marginally to 99.21. The bond market's hawkish repricing is the dominant cross-asset signal — yields rising alongside oil signals a stagflationary impulse that compresses equity valuations.

singapore.summary: The Singapore banks grind higher but remain mechanically blocked. DBS (+6.8%) and OCBC (+6.1%) are overbought on thin volume, while UOB (+2.6%) is nearest to its 50-day but still constrained by the NIM‑compression regime. US10Y at 4.59% keeps the dual‑channel headwind intact — no relief until either the Fed cuts or MAS eases. OCBC outperformed in Q1 profit growth, but that edge is already priced in.
singapore.thesis_status: Structurally bearish — no NIM catalyst expected before MAS October review.
singapore.key_development: US10Y yields rose further to 4.59% on Monday, extending the period above the 4.20% threshold that would ease USD funding costs.

semiconductors.summary: The semis cluster suffered a broad 3.9% drop (SOXX at 508.52) as the Trump‑Xi summit yielded no chip deal and bond yields surged. Every name remains on CAUTION, with NVDA, AMD, MU, and AVGO all more than 15% above their 50-day averages. The internal divergence is stark: TSEM and LITE hold gains on silicon photonics deals, while MU and AMD are off sharply. The cluster is structurally bullish — AI capex is intact — but tactically extended and vulnerable to a rate‑driven de‑rating.
semiconductors.thesis_status: Structurally bullish, tactically extended — no entry until yields stabilise or a pullback resets R:R.
semiconductors.key_development: The Trump‑Xi summit's failure to address chip export controls and the 30-year Treasury auction above 5% combined to push SOXX -3.9%, its worst day in two weeks.

big_tech.summary: BigTech was mixed: MSFT (+3.4%) rebounded on AI bargain narratives, while AMZN (-0.9%) and GOOG (-0.9%) slipped. All four names remain CAUTION, with GOOG the most extended (+18.9%, RSI 70) after Druckenmiller's exit. The cluster's structural story — hyperscaler AI capex — is intact, but valuation compression from rising yields is the near‑term headwind. MSFT's Azure AI demand signal remains the cleanest read, but even it needs a pullback to the 50-day for entry.
big_tech.thesis_status: Structurally bullish but rate‑sensitive — AI capex visibility battles rising discount rates.
big_tech.key_development: MSFT's +3.4% gain on Barchart's 'AI bargain' thesis and Bill Gates' exit from the stock added noise; the core thesis rests on non‑OpenAI Azure RPO growth, not Gates.

interconnected.entry_note: Add to watchlist if NVDA earnings May 21 highlight photonics demand or if COHR pulls back 15% from current levels.

interconnected.entry_note: Add to watchlist if a new Pentagon contract or escalation news pushes the stock below the 50-day average.

interconnected.name: Snap
interconnected.reason: Advertising‑dependent tech; a hawkish Fed tightening cycle compresses ad budgets, making SNAP a leveraged short candidate for the BigTech cluster.
interconnected.entry_note: Add to watchlist if the 10‑year yield breaks above 4.75% and ad‑tech multiples contract sharply.

interconnected.name: ChargePoint
interconnected.reason: Oil at $103/bbl benefits EV infrastructure stocks as fuel costs rise; connected to the AI power bottleneck theme (BE).
interconnected.entry_note: Add to watchlist if WTI holds above $110 for a week, accelerating fleet electrification talk.

interconnected.name: Tesla
interconnected.reason: FSD v14.3 rollout progress (per Not a Tesla App, May 16) and China tariff talks could improve Tesla's market access.
interconnected.entry_note: Add to watchlist if China tariff cuts on EVs are finalized or FSD subscription uptake beats Q2 estimates.

active_risks: Hormuz blockade enters week 4 with new ship seizures; NSC war cabinet meets May 19 with Saudi airspace veto risk.
Fed rate hike odds surge above 50% after CPI 3.8% and PPI 6.0%; Warsh faces market test of hawkish credibility.
US-China trade talks yield tariff reduction signals but no chip export control breakthrough; rare‑earth access improved.
Global bond rout deepens as 30‑year yield exceeds 5% at auction, raising sovereign funding costs and compressing equity multiples.
geopolitical.market_impact: Oil at $103 and 10‑year at 4.59% are a stagflationary drag on growth equities. Semis (NVDA, AMD, MU) sold off as the China summit failed to deliver chip relief, while Singapore banks (DBS, OCBC) remain pinned by elevated funding costs. The bond sell‑off is the dominant risk — if yields break higher, even AI structural stories will de‑rate.
geopolitical.new_since_yesterday: The NSC war cabinet meeting on May 19 (per House of Saud, May 18) raised the probability of US military escalation against Iran, with Saudi airspace veto a live operational risk.
geopolitical.portfolio_action: Hold cash; no new entries until the NSC meeting outcome is known and the bond market stabilises. Selective entry on oversold semis only after a meaningful pullback.

portfolio_snapshot.risk_posture: Twenty‑one of 22 names are hard‑blocked on technical extension or RSI — only WRD offers a clean entry setup.
